metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1738
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig. 5D, LTP of synapses between C-fibers and dorsal horn neurons in mice in vivo is impaired in EPHB1 KO mice.

Literal:
"our results showed that targeted mutation of the EphB1 receptor completely abolished the LTP in all mice tested (n = 9) (Fig. 5D). These genetic data strongly suggest that this EphB1 receptor is the specific Eph family receptor critical to and required for development of LTP."

Fig. 6E-H, high frequency stimulation induced increases of p-CaMKII, p-ERK and p-CREB in dorsal horn neurons is abolished of EPHB1 KO mice.

Literal:
"Further, targeted mutation of the EphB1 receptor completely blocked HFS induced increases of p-CaMKII, p-ERK (Fig. 6E, F) and p-CREB (Fig. 6G, H)."

and Fig. 7, high frequency stimulation induced increases c-Fos expression is reduced in in dorsal horn neurons is abolished of EPHB1 KO mice.

Literal:
"Similarly, HFS-induced c-Fos expression was significantly reduced by EphB2-Fc (2 μg, from 30 min prior to 3 h after HFS) in WT mice, or prevented by targeted mutation of the EphB1 receptor in the EphB1-/- mice. Representative photomicrographs and the corresponding counts of Fos-like immunoreactive neurons in DH are shown in Fig. 7.. Experimental description: The EPHB1 KO mice were used.

Literal form M&M:
"The generation of EphB1 receptor protein-null mutant mice has been previously described [51,52]. For the present study EphB1+/- heterozygous males and females in the CD1 background were bred to obtain a cohort of homozygous knockout (EphB1-/-) and wild-type (WT) (EphB1+/+) control littermate adult mice (25-30 g-wt)."
